LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS: A) GRANTED AS TO ALL ISSUES THAT THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION DOES NOT STATE A COGNIZABLE CLAIM (ISSUES 1-5) WITH LEAVE TO AMEND GIVEN TO PLAINTIFFS TO ALLEGE A NEW SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO PAY WAGES FOUNDED ON LABOR CODE 1194 AND B) DENIED AS TO ALL STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ISSUES (ISSUES 6-8), INSOFAR AS THOSE CAUSES OF ACTION PERTAIN TO THE CLAIMS ASSERTED IN PLAINTIFFS ORIGINAL COMPLAINT. THE PARTIES AGREE THAT THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION RELIES ON A STATUTE THAT DOES NOT CARRY A PRIVATE CAUSE OF ACTION. BECAUSE OF CALIFORNIA'S LIBERAL POLICY ALLOWING AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS, PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO CORRECT THIS DEFICIENCY BY ALLEGING A NEW SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION PREDICTED ON LABOR CODE 1194. (BOSTROM V. COUNTY OF SAN BERNARDINO (1995) 35 CAL.APP.4TH 1654, 1663). HOWEVER, TO AVOID PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT, THE TRIAL DATE IS VACATED AND DISCOVERY IS REOPENED ONLY WITH RESPECT TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFFS' PROPOSED AMENDED COMPLAINT. WITH RESPECT TO ISSUES 6, 7, AND 8, THE APPLICABLE STATUTES OF LIMITATIONS WERE TOLLED BY THE EARLIER CLASS ACTION WHERE PLAINTIFFS SPECIFICALLY ALLEGED THEY WERE NOT PAID MINIMUM WAGES AND WERE DENIED MEAL PERIODS. THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS FOR MR. PRINGLE'S FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION WAS LIKEWISE TOLLED BY THE EARLIER CLASS ACTION AND THE TOLLING WAS UNAFFECTED BY MR. PRINGLE'S ATTEMPT TO RECEIVE ADMINISTRATIVE RELIEF. (ELKINS V. DERBY (1978) 12 CAL.3D 410, 414). HOWEVER, THIS RULING DOES NOT APPLY TO PLAINTIFFS' NEWLY ASSERTED SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IN THEIR PROPOSED AMENDED COMPLAINT. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Sep-30-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorneys, Laura Herron Weber and J. Wynne Herron of Herron & Herron (415.788.2500) appear as counsels for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, George A. Sthner of Faegre Baker Daniels (310.500.2124) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

Attorney, Jennifer S. Abramowitz of AT&T Services Inc. (310.964.5201) appears as in-house counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is: a) granted as to all issues that the second cause of action does not state a cognizable claim (issues 1-5) with leave to amend given to plaintiffs to allege a new second cause of action for failure to pay wages founded on Labor Code 1194 and b) denied as to all statute of limitations issues (issues 6-8), insofar as those causes of action pertain to the claims asserted in plaintiffs original complaint. The parties agree that the second cause of action relies on a statute that does not carry a private cause of action. Because of California's liberal policy allowing amendments to pleadings, plaintiffs are given leave to correct this deficiency by alleging a new second cause of action predicted on Labor Code 1194. (Bostrom v. County of San Bernardino (1995) 35 Cal.App.4th 1654, 1663). However, to avoid prejudice to Defendant, the trial date is vacated and discovery is reopened only with respect to the second cause of action in Plaintiffs' proposed Amended Complaint. With respect to issues 6, 7, and 8, the applicable statutes of limitations were tolled by the earlier class action where plaintiffs specifically alleged they were not paid minimum wages and were denied meal periods. The statute of limitations for Mr. Pringle's first cause of action was likewise tolled by the earlier class action and the tolling was unaffected by Mr. Pringle's attempt to receive administrative relief. (Elkins v. Derby (1978) 12 Cal.3d 410, 414). However, this ruling does not apply to Plaintiffs' newly asserted second cause of action in their proposed Amended Complaint. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
